stories.title: Spurs 118-91 Thunder — Wembanyama Dominates Game 6, Forces Game 7
stories.angle: Wembanyama's dominance forces the ultimate showdown; SGA's worst game of the series.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Wemby had 28 PTS / 10 REB / 3 BLK and the Spurs blasted the Thunder 118-91 in Game 6.

SGA shot 6-of-18. -28 rating.

GAME 7. Saturday. OKC. 🏀

#Spurs #Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
SGA — the back-to-back MVP — shot 6-of-18 for 15 pts and finished -28 in Game 6.

The Thunder need their star to show up Saturday.

Home. Paycom. 7 PM ET. NBC.

#Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
WEMBY FORCES GAME 7.

The San Antonio Spurs dismantled the Oklahoma City Thunder 118-91 in Game 6 last night at AT&T Center, and Victor Wembanyama was absolutely magnificent.

Wembanyama finished with 28 points, 10 rebounds, 3 blocks, and 2 steals — shooting 4-of-9 from three-point range. He played 28 dominant minutes before the game was so out of hand that his coaches sat him with 9:20 remaining.

The turning point: a savage 20-0 run in the third quarter. The Thunder went scoreless for 7 straight minutes. The Spurs outscored OKC 32-13 in the third to turn a competitive game into a rout.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander — the reigning back-to-back MVP — had a nightmare: 15 points on 6-of-18 shooting, a game-worst -28 rating.

Series tied 3-3. GAME 7 is Saturday night, 7 PM ET on NBC/Peacock, at the Paycom Center in Oklahoma City.

Can SGA bounce back at home? Or will Wembanyama silence the crowd and send SA to the NBA Finals?

Drop your prediction below! Will it be OKC or SA in the Finals?
facebook.image_caption:
GAME 6 BY THE NUMBERS
Wembanyama: 28 PTS | 10 REB | 3 BLK | 2 STL
SGA: 15 PTS | 6/18 FG | -28 rating
Spurs 118 – Thunder 91. GAME 7 Saturday. #NBAPlayoffs #HoopHeroes

stories.title: WCF Game 7 — Thunder vs. Spurs, Saturday Night, Paycom Center
stories.angle: Winner-take-all showdown; Thunder's home dominance vs. Spurs' road toughness.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Game 7. Winner-take-all.

Thunder -3.5 at home (6-1 in OKC this postseason).
Spurs +3.5 (5-3 on the road).

Saturday. 7 PM ET. NBC/Peacock.

Who advances to the NBA Finals? 👇

#Thunder #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
No. 1 Thunder or No. 2 Spurs — whoever wins Game 7 gets home court vs. the Knicks in the NBA Finals.

OKC: 34-7 at home this season.
SA: 62-20 overall.

Knicks (53-29) just have to watch. 👀

#NBAPlayoffs #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
THE ULTIMATE GAME 7 IS SET.

Winner-take-all. One game. NBA Finals on the line.

After the Spurs' dominant 118-91 win in Game 6, the Thunder and Spurs will settle the Western Conference Finals on Saturday night (May 30, 7 PM ET, NBC/Peacock) at the Paycom Center in Oklahoma City.

Here's what you need to know:

Home Court: Oklahoma City was 34-7 at home in the regular season — the best home record in the NBA. In this postseason, they're 6-1 at Paycom Center. This is a massive advantage.

Spurs Road Record: San Antonio is 5-3 on the road during these playoffs. They're battle-tested.

The Odds: Thunder are 3.5-point favorites at -155 on the moneyline. Spurs are +130 underdogs. Total: 212.5 points.

What's at Stake: The winner faces the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals starting June 3 — AND gets home court advantage. Both the Thunder (64-18) and Spurs (62-20) had better regular season records than the Knicks (53-29).

SGA needs to be SGA again. Wembanyama needs to do what he does. Someone's season ends Saturday night.

Who do you have in Game 7?
facebook.image_caption:
GAME 7. SATURDAY. 7 PM ET. OKC. NBC/PEACOCK.
Thunder -3.5 (6-1 at home this postseason)
Spurs +3.5 (5-3 on the road)
The winner faces the Knicks in the NBA Finals. Who's going? #Thunder #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: SGA's Redemption Arc — Back-to-Back MVP Must Respond
stories.angle: Great players have short memories. SGA at Paycom Center with the Finals on the line.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
SGA has been the best player alive in this postseason.

Game 6? 6-for-18. -28 rating.

Game 7 at Paycom is his chance to remind everyone why he won back-to-back MVP.

#Thunder #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
THE GAME SHAI NEEDS TO FORGET.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander is one of the best players on the planet. He just won back-to-back MVP awards with a 31.1-point regular season average. He's the engine of the Thunder's championship run.

And then came Game 6.

SGA scored just 15 points on 6-of-18 shooting. His plus/minus: a game-worst -28. The Spurs were everywhere he went, and Victor Wembanyama's length erased anything SGA tried to create near the rim.

But here's the thing about great players — they respond.

Game 7 is Saturday at home, in Oklahoma City, in front of a crowd that will be absolutely electric. SGA has been brilliant at Paycom Center this postseason. The Thunder are 6-1 at home in these playoffs when SGA is at his best.

One bad game doesn't define a career. A Game 7 at home, with the NBA Finals on the line, can define a legacy.

Can the back-to-back MVP deliver when it matters most? We find out Saturday night.
facebook.image_caption:
SGA IS COMING FOR REVENGE.
Game 6: 15 pts | 6/18 FG | -28 rating
Game 7: Home. Paycom. Saturday. 7 PM ET.
The best players respond. #Thunder #SGA #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: SGA's Redemption Moment: The Back-to-Back MVP Needs a Game 7 Response

stories.title: Knicks Watch — Finals Schedule, Home Court, and 27 Years of Waiting
stories.angle: 27-year wait for New York — Finals opponent and home court question finally gets answered Game 7 Saturday.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Knicks haven't been in the NBA Finals since 1999.

They've been resting for 12+ days while OKC and SA go to war in Game 7 tomorrow.

Finals G1: June 3. ABC. 8:30 PM ET.

The wait continues. 🏆

#Knicks #NBAFinals #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
THE KNICKS ARE WATCHING. AND WAITING.

New York hasn't been in the NBA Finals since 1999 — 27 long years. They swept the Cleveland Cavaliers in four games, riding an 11-game playoff win streak, and now they're watching while Oklahoma City and San Antonio play Game 7.

Here's what Knicks fans need to know about the Finals picture:

FINALS SCHEDULE:
Game 1: June 3, 8:30 PM ET — ABC
Game 2: June 5, 8:30 PM ET — ABC
Game 3: June 8, 8:30 PM ET — ABC
Game 4: June 10, 8:30 PM ET — ABC
Game 5: June 13 (if needed) — ABC

HOME COURT: The Western Conference winner (OKC at 64-18 or SA at 62-20) gets home court over the Knicks (53-29). New York will be the away team for Games 1 and 2.

THE ADVANTAGE: All that rest could be a massive equalizer. The Knicks are fresh, healthy, and ready. Their only question is rust.

The wait is almost over. MSG will be deafening when the Finals begin.

Are the Knicks going to win it all? Drop your prediction! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption:
KNICKS NBA FINALS SCHEDULE
G1 - June 3 | 8:30 PM ET | ABC
G2 - June 5 | 8:30 PM ET | ABC
G3 - June 8 | 8:30 PM ET | ABC
27 years in the making. This is it, New York. #Knicks #NBAFinals

stories.title: Wembanyama's WCF Statistical Masterclass — Age 22, No Ceiling
stories.angle: Generational brilliance — the greatest Conference Finals performance at age 22?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama in the 2026 WCF:

G1: 41 PTS / 24 REB / 3 BLK (2OT)
G6: 28 PTS / 10 REB / 3 BLK

Age 22. Unanimous DPOY. No ceiling in sight.

#Spurs #NBAPlayoffs #Wembanyama

facebook.long_form:
WEMBANYAMA IS FROM ANOTHER DIMENSION.

Let's take a moment to appreciate what Victor Wembanyama has done in the 2026 Western Conference Finals:

Game 1 (2OT WIN): 41 pts, 24 reb, 3 blk — joined Wilt Chamberlain as the only players with 40+ pts and 20+ reb in a Conference Finals debut.

Game 6 (118-91 WIN): 28 pts, 10 reb, 3 blk, 2 stl — shot 4-of-9 from three and made it look easy before being sat with the game decided.

He's 22 years old. He won the Defensive Player of the Year award unanimously (100/100 first-place votes) — the first player ever to do so. He led the NBA in blocks for the second straight season.

If the Spurs make the Finals, a huge part of the story will be this 7-foot-4 player from France who's rewriting the record books at age 22.

Watch him Saturday night. 🏀
facebook.image_caption:
AGE 22. UNANIMOUS DPOY. WCF MONSTER.
G1: 41 PTS / 24 REB / 3 BLK
G6: 28 PTS / 10 REB / 3 BLK / 2 STL
Victor Wembanyama is DIFFERENT. #Wembanyama #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs
